goal: is_lower_bound(p, a) = forall(x0: L) { p(x0) implies a <= x0 }
proof: function[T0: LinearOrder](x0: T0 -> Bool, x1: T0) { forall(x2: T0) { not x0(x2) or x1 <= x2 } = is_lower_bound[T0](x0, x1) }[L](p, a)
p(x)
is_lower_bound(p, a)
(forall(x0: L) { not p(x0) or a <= x0 } = true)
function(x0: L) { not (p(x0) and not a <= x0) }(x)

goal: is_upper_bound(p, b) = forall(x0: L) { p(x0) implies x0 <= b }
proof: function[T0: LinearOrder](x0: T0 -> Bool, x1: T0) { forall(x2: T0) { not x0(x2) or x2 <= x1 } = is_upper_bound[T0](x0, x1) }[L](p, b)
p(x)
is_upper_bound(p, b)
(forall(x0: L) { not p(x0) or x0 <= b } = true)
function(x0: L) { not (p(x0) and not x0 <= b) }(x)

goal: is_bounded_by_interval(p, a, b) = forall(x0: L) { p(x0) implies closed_interval(a, b, x0) }
proof: function[T0: LinearOrder](x0: T0 -> Bool, x1: T0, x2: T0) { forall(x3: T0) { not x0(x3) or closed_interval[T0](x1, x2, x3) } = is_bounded_by_interval[T0](x0, x1, x2) }[L](p, a, b)
p(x)
is_bounded_by_interval(p, a, b)
(forall(x0: L) { not p(x0) or closed_interval(a, b, x0) } = true)
function(x0: L) { not (p(x0) and not closed_interval(a, b, x0)) }(x)
function[T0: LinearOrder](x0: T0, x1: T0, x2: T0) { not x0 <= x1 or not x1 <= x2 or closed_interval[T0](x0, x2, x1) }[L](a, x, b)
function[T0: LinearOrder](x0: T0 -> Bool, x1: T0, x2: T0) { forall(x3: T0) { not x0(x3) or closed_interval[T0](x1, x2, x3) } = is_bounded_by_interval[T0](x0, x1, x2) }[L](p, a, b)
function[T0: LinearOrder](x0: T0, x1: T0, x2: T0) { not closed_interval[T0](x0, x1, x2) or x0 <= x2 }[L](a, b, x)
function[T0: LinearOrder](x0: T0 -> Bool, x1: T0) { forall(x2: T0) { not x0(x2) or x1 <= x2 } = is_lower_bound[T0](x0, x1) }[L](p, a)
function[T0: LinearOrder](x0: T0, x1: T0, x2: T0) { not closed_interval[T0](x0, x1, x2) or x2 <= x1 }[L](a, b, x)
function[T0: LinearOrder](x0: T0 -> Bool, x1: T0) { forall(x2: T0) { not x0(x2) or x2 <= x1 } = is_upper_bound[T0](x0, x1) }[L](p, b)

goal: is_bounded_by_interval(p, a, b) = (is_lower_bound(p, a) and is_upper_bound(p, b))
exists(k0: L, k1: L) { k0 = a and k1 = b and is_bounded_by_interval(p, k0, k1) }

proof: function[T0: LinearOrder](x0: T0 -> Bool) { exists(k0: T0, k1: T0) { is_bounded_by_interval[T0](x0, k0, k1) } = is_bounded[T0](x0) }[L](p)
not exists(k0: L, k1: L) { is_bounded_by_interval(p, k0, k1) }
function(x0: L) { not exists(k0: L) { is_bounded_by_interval(p, x0, k0) } }(a)
function(x0: L, x1: L) { not is_bounded_by_interval(p, x0, x1) }(a, b)
